We're seeing a large production impact starting ~08:45 PDT: inference requests to your hosted endpoint are returning 502/504 at scale and p99 token latency has doubled. Our SLO alerts fired across multiple clusters; attached is the alert export. This is affecting customers on the live tier.

- A subset of requests hang for ~20s before 504s.

text:
From: ops@brightroot.com
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Jun 2029 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: High 5xx rate and throughput collapse across eu-us peering

Avery —

We're seeing a sharp increase in 5xxs and a 60-80% throughput drop starting ~07:55 PDT. Traces point at the VPC peering path to your eu-west transit (AS4455) where packets are being retransmitted and responses delayed. We also saw DNS timeouts where our resolvers report TTLs of 1s and a flood of A queries.

Attached: broot-debug.zip (pcap + traces + app logs)

Immediate impact: checkout flow failures for ~30% of users in US-east. We're hearing from product that this is hitting checkout conversions and potentially crossing our SLA.

Can you confirm whether any firewall rule/action, peering change, or DNS config rollou...

From: Claire Chen <claire@emberlynx.ai>

Cc: Marcus Li <marcus@emberlynx.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 9 Apr 2029 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot extension — request for additional credits + runway

Ben — thanks again for the demo last week. EmberLynx's engineering team is seeing promising results but needs a bit more runway to validate accuracy at scale. Two asks: 

1) Top-up the pilot credits by 150k tokens (we burned through the initial allocation faster than expected due to longer context tests).
2) Extend the private trial by an additional 30 days so we can run the VPC-based integration and finish compliance review.

Subject: Short window patch required for scheduler jitter (customer-facing note drafted)

Team —

We found a narrow but reproducible regression in the...
text:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-11-14T08:12:00-08:00
Subject: Short window patch required for scheduler jitter (customer-facing note drafted)

Team —

We found a narrow but reproducible regression in the request scheduler that intermittently increases tail latency when clients send many concurrent short streaming requests (multi-token streaming bursts). Root cause: race in the slot reclamation path under high concurrent interleaving. Impact is limited but visible on a subset of customers (notably Acme Retail) as 200–400ms spikes on tail p50->p99 for short requests.

Planned immediate mitigation: a lightweight hotfix to force a deterministic slot release path and tighten the reclamation lock window. This is a short, in-process patch (no model reload) that we can deploy clus...

text:
From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@novatech.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 9 Nov 2028 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: NOVA - sudden increase in latency and errors since last canary

Alex —

We started seeing a significant degradation in inference performance across several endpoints shortly after your team flipped the canary yesterday afternoon. Our P95 jumped from ~110ms to ~480ms and P99s are consistently 1.4–1.9s. Error rates climbed from 0.2% to ~3% during heavy tails. This is impacting synchronous search flows; downstream timeouts have triggered customer complaints.

We rolled back our own client-side retries but the issue tracks to your hosted endpoints (region us-west2). The spike aligns with the canary timestamp: Nov 9 14:40 PST. Attached are a small sample of traces and our aggregated latency metrics.

We need an immediate mitigation plan and timeline for a rollba...

text:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>

Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 04 Oct 2028 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo recap + wiring diagram proposal

Hi Ethan / Priya —

Thanks again for taking the demo this morning. Quick recap and a proposed "wiring diagram" to make the POC procurement path as predictable as possible. High-level notes from our side: 

- Success criteria we heard: (1) 99th pct latency < 500ms for chat endpoints; (2) automated logging of token-level usage for billing and audit; (3) data residency limited to US-east region.

Hi Morgan / Acme infra team —

We’re seeing a targeted capacity/egress degradation affecting requests routed to eu-west-1 and ap-southeast-1 in the last 45 minutes. Symptoms we observe: elevated 5xx rate (predominantly 502/504), increased p99 latency for generation calls, and intermittent KV fetch timeouts for a subset of hosts. Impact is limited to a subset of customers behind specific cloud load balancer paths; US regions are nominal right now.

Immediate mitigation steps (what you can do right now):
- Prefer region override: set inference.region=us-west-2 or us-east-1 in your Redw...
